title: ranunculus sychnodon
parts: Ranunculus sychnodon has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of the plant contain alkaloids like ranunculin and sychnodine, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of Ranunculus sychnodon contain flavonoids and terpenoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The flowers of the plant contain glycosides and phenolic compounds, such as ranunculin and apigenin, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
side_effects: Ranunculus sychnodon can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in large doses due to its toxic glycosides. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can also cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering. In severe cases, ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can lead to renal failure and liver damage. Additionally, improper handling of the plant can cause allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis in rare instances.
